checklist.question: Did receivables grow materially faster than revenue across consecutive periods?

evidence.quote: ReceivablesNetCurrent=23,020,000 (2016-06-30) vs ReceivablesNetCurrent=19,518,000 (2015-06-30); SalesRevenueNet Q2 2016=37,902,000 vs Q2 2015=36,499,000

checklist.question: Did non-current 'soft' assets (intangibles, other assets, capitalized items) grow materially faster than the business overall?
Is there a persistent divergence between net income and operating cash flow?

evidence.location: 10-K Statement of Cash Flows / Income Statement, FY2015

checklist.question: Do inventory levels or gross margins show trends inconsistent with revenue?

evidence.quote: InventoryNet=8,044,336 (2010-12-31) growing to InventoryNet=14,202,000 (2015-12-31), a 76.6% increase
SalesRevenueNet=120,766,450 (FY2010) growing to SalesRevenueNet=138,850,000 (FY2015), only a 15.0% increase
evidence.location: 10-K Income Statement, FY2010 vs FY2015 revenue

evidence.quote: GrossProfit=41,014,000 on SalesRevenueNet=139,223,000 (FY2013, margin 29.5%) declined to GrossProfit=36,880,000 on SalesRevenueNet=139,307,000 (FY2014, margin 26.5%)
evidence.location: 10-K Income Statement, FY2013 vs FY2014 gross margin

checklist.question: Is the earnings trajectory unusually smooth relative to the volatility of revenue and cash flow?

evidence.quote: NetIncomeLoss quarterly values ranged from 1,075,000 (Q1 2016) to 2,735,000 (Q2 2016), a ~150% swing, while SalesRevenueNet ranged 34,503,000 to 37,902,000 (~10% swing) in the same quarters
NetCashProvidedByUsedInOperatingActivitiesContinuingOperations=-2,408,000 (Q1 2016) vs positive NetIncomeLoss=1,075,000 same quarter, showing real divergence in volatility not smoothed away

checklist.question: Did liability, allowance, or reserve balances decline (or fail to grow) despite growing business activity?

evidence.quote: AllowanceForDoubtfulAccountsReceivableCurrent=378,859 (2011-12-31, 2.43% of receivables) grew to AllowanceForDoubtfulAccountsReceivableCurrent=499,000 (2015-12-31, 2.85% of receivables) and 597,000 (2016-09-30, 2.68% of receivables)
evidence.location: 10-Q/10-K Balance Sheet, allowance for doubtful accounts

checklist.question: Does the filing chronology show irregularities (late filings/NT forms, amendments, unusual 8-K frequency)?

evidence.quote: 10-K filed 2016-03-11 followed roughly five months later by a 10-K/A filed 2016-08-18, with a CORRESP letter also filed on 2016-08-18

checklist.question: Is the provided data sufficient in coverage and length for the above assessments?

evidence.quote: Financial series data spans annual and quarterly points from FY2008/2009 through Q3 2016 across Assets, Revenue, Receivables, Inventory, Cash Flow, and related concepts, enabling multi-year trend analysis

mechanism_hypotheses.accounting_treatment: Possible insufficient inventory reserves/write-downs for slow-moving or obsolete inventory as inventory grew 76.6% (InventoryNet=8,044,336 in FY2010 to InventoryNet=14,202,000 in FY2015) far outpacing 15.0% revenue growth (SalesRevenueNet=120,766,450 FY2010 to SalesRevenueNet=138,850,000 FY2015), coinciding with a gross margin decline from 29.5% (FY2013: GrossProfit=41,014,000/SalesRevenueNet=139,223,000) to 26.5% (FY2014: GrossProfit=36,880,000/SalesRevenueNet=139,307,000). This pattern is consistent with inventory being carried above net realizable value, understating COGS and overstating gross profit/inventory asset value in the near term.

rationale_evidence.location: Income Statement, gross margin FY2013 vs FY2014

mechanism_hypotheses.accounting_treatment: Possible premature revenue recognition, extended customer payment terms, or channel-stuffing in fiscal 2016, as accounts receivable grew 11.8%-17.9% year-over-year in Q1-Q3 2016 while revenue grew only 1.5%-8.1% in the same quarters, suggesting revenue may be overstated relative to actual cash collections or receivables quality may be deteriorating without a corresponding increase in the allowance for doubtful accounts.

rationale_evidence.quote: ReceivablesNetCurrent=22,307,000 (2016-09-30) vs ReceivablesNetCurrent=19,255,000 (2015-09-30); SalesRevenueNet=37,220,000 (Q3 2016) vs SalesRevenueNet=34,441,000 (Q3 2015)
rationale_evidence.location: 10-Q Balance Sheet/Income Statement, Q3 2016 vs Q3 2015

rationale_evidence.quote: ReceivablesNetCurrent=23,020,000 (2016-06-30) vs ReceivablesNetCurrent=19,518,000 (2015-06-30); SalesRevenueNet=37,902,000 (Q2 2016) vs SalesRevenueNet=36,499,000 (Q2 2015)
rationale_evidence.location: 10-Q Balance Sheet/Income Statement, Q2 2016 vs Q2 2015

mechanism_hypotheses.accounting_treatment: A 10-K/A filed on 2016-08-18, approximately five months after the original FY2015 10-K (filed 2016-03-11), accompanied by a CORRESP letter on the same date, suggests the company may have needed to amend or clarify prior-period financial disclosures in response to an SEC review, which could indicate an underlying error or non-compliant accounting treatment in the original filing that required correction.

top_signals: Inventory (InventoryNet) grew 76.6% from FY2010 to FY2015 while revenue grew only 15.0%, coinciding with a gross margin decline from 29.5% (FY2013) to 26.5% (FY2014), suggesting potential inventory valuation/reserve issues
Accounts receivable growth substantially outpaced revenue growth in Q1-Q3 2016 (AR +11.8% to +17.9% YoY vs revenue +1.5% to +8.1% YoY), raising possible revenue quality/collectability concerns

A 10-K/A amendment was filed on 2016-08-18, about five months after the original FY2015 10-K, together with a CORRESP letter the same day, indicating possible SEC-driven correction to prior financial disclosures
Operating cash flow consistently exceeded net income across the annual periods examined, which is a mitigating factor reducing overall earnings-quality concern
